---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Caspar Leung"
date: "2026-03-05"
output: html_document
---


```{r initialization}
library(ggplot2)

# Read the file skipping the first 2 rows
df <- read.csv("multiTimeline.csv", skip = 2, header = TRUE)

# Rename columns
colnames(df) <- c("Time", "Interest")

df$Time <- substr(df$Time, 1, 19)
df$Time <- as.POSIXct(df$Time, format="%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")

# Check the first few rows
head(df)
```

```{r}
#Interest vs time plot
ggplot(df, aes(x = Time, y = Interest)) +
  geom_line(color = "gray60", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue", size = 1, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "darkred", se = FALSE, linewidth = 1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_datetime(
    date_labels = "%m-%d %H:%M", 
    date_breaks = "6 hours"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "BTS Interest: Points, Raw Line, and Trend",
    x = "Time of Day",
    y = "Interest Score"
  )

```
```{r}
#histogram distribution of search interest
ggplot(df, aes(x = Interest)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 15, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of BTS Search Interest",
    subtitle = "Frequency of interest scores across the recorded period",
    x = "Interest Score (0-100)",
    y = "Frequency"
  )

```
```{r}
#ACF PACF plot
par(mfrow=c(1,2)) # Put them side-by-side
acf(df$Interest, main="ACF ")
pacf(df$Interest, main="PACF ")
par(mfrow=c(1,1)) # Reset layout

```
